Two More Breaches. Two More Lessons About Third-Party Risk.  Hims & Hers, the telehealth company, disclosed that a February cyberattack compromised a customer-support system, exposing personal information across multiple states.  Mister Guns, a Texas-based gun retailer, is notifying 21,225 people that a November hack exposed names, Social Security numbers, concealed handgun licenses, and other sensitive personal data.  These incidents share a common thread: attackers targeted supporting systems, not just core databases, to get what they needed.   Customer-support platforms, CRM tools, and third-party integrations are frequently the weakest link in an organization's security posture. Adversaries know this. Most detection tools are tuned for the core network, not the edges. MITRE ATT&CK: Hardware Recon (T1592.001)  Before an attacker strikes, they study your physical infrastructure. From servers to biometric systems, knowing your gear helps them find the perfect exploit.  How to stay safe:     Limit public info: Be careful with network diagrams and job post details.     Audit your footprint: Know what hardware details are visible to active scans. Supply chain attack on Axios npm package exposed 100 million weekly downloads to platform-specific RATs. Malicious versions 1.14.1 and 0.30.4 deployed March 31, 2026 for 3-hour window before detection.  Technical breakdown: • Fake dependency "plain-crypto-js" executes post-install, contacts 142[.]11[.]206[.]73 for OS-specific payload delivery • Windows: Downloads PS1, copies powershell.exe to %PROGRAMDATA%\wt.exe, executes with bypass flags • MacOS: Deploys "com.apple.act.mond" binary via zsh execution • Linux: Python-based RAT with remote access capabilities • Attack vector: Supply chain compromise (T1195.002) with automatic payload deployment  DFIR teams should immediately: - Hunt for plain-crypto-js dependency in package.json/node_modules - Monitor for network connections to 142[.]11[.]206[.]73 and sfrclak[.]com - Check %PROGRAMDATA%\wt.exe on Windows systems - Scan for the provided SHA256 hashes across endpoints - Rotate all credentials on affected systems  Organizations using Axios should rollback to v1.14.0/v0.30.3 and assume credential compromise if malicious versions were downloaded. Full IOC list available in Cisco Talos report.  #DFIR_Radar

‼️🇨🇱 Zyght, a HSE (Health, Safety & Environment) software platform, has allegedly been breached with 6.1TB of data put up for sale on a popular cybercrime forum.  Threat Actor: igotafeeling Category: Breach Victim: Zyght Industry: HSE Software Site: zyght.com  The threat actor claims to have obtained 6.1TB of customer uploads and the full source code for Zyght's HSE software. The company was allegedly aware of the breach for weeks and did not resolve it privately.  Affected companies include major corporations such as: Codelco, Nestlé, Mutual de Seguridad CChC, Chilquinta Energía, SQM, Glencore, EcoMetales, Trafigura, Albemarle, SAAM, GHL Hoteles, Fénix Power Perú, Banco de Chile, Grupo México, Falabella, Fresnillo plc, Industrias Peñoles, ASARCO, Goldfields, and many more across mining, energy, banking, and retail sectors.  Exposed data includes worker PII and medical records, incident/accident files, identity documents, training and competency records, site security info, environmental compliance, audit and legal documents, and PPE/asset records.  Customer buckets listed include: Codelco (769.4GB), gssodet (629.1GB), Codelco-prepro (475.6GB), gsoch (283.4GB), Mutual (281.0GB), Chilquinta (232.1GB), Nestlé (201.9GB), SQM (191.9GB), Disal (163.5GB), and Goldfields (145.6GB), among others.  The data will be sold strictly once after a one-week deadline (April 10 GMT-3), giving affected companies a chance to reach out first, per the actor.

sample_tweets.text: 🚨 Crocodilus Android Banking Trojan v1.8.2 Advertised with Advanced Remote Access and Crypto Theft Capabilities 🚨  A threat actor is advertising “Crocodilus” version 1.8.2, an advanced Android banking trojan targeting devices from Android 8 to Android 16. The malware is promoted with extensive surveillance and control capabilities, including H-VNC functionality with black screen support, full screen sharing on newer Android versions, and bypass techniques aimed at accessibility-based protections used by banking applications. The bot is capable of harvesting sensitive data such as SMS messages, 2FA authentication codes, screen unlock credentials, and crypto wallet seed phrases, while also enabling real-time manipulation of wallet addresses during transactions. Additional features include keylogging, call redirection, silent app execution, and disabling security mechanisms such as Google Play Protect. The infrastructure includes a management panel with builder and dropper capabilities, encrypted payload delivery, and fast-flux support for resilient command-and-control operations.  #AndroidMalware #DarkWeb #CyberCrime #ThreatIntel

Experiencing some pretty hardcore burn out in malware.  However, a word of advice for the noobs, or less-er experienced people in cybersecurity, "burn out" is part of the natural progression of this ecosystem and it happens to everyone.  Your brain is a muscle (not literally, but brains have this dumb stuff called neuroplasticity, some nerd stuff, whatever), and just like a muscle, you need down time to heal, and science, or something.  Myself personally, I tend to go through waves of absurd productivity with little to no pacing. I get extremely excited, rip through code, ... and then lose control and crash and burn.  Then it takes me anywhere between a few days, ... or few weeks, ... or worse case a few months to recompose myself and get back in the game.  This is a good opportunity to switch it up a little bit. Instead of going schizo on malware, I've been exploring the internet, reading about current geopolitical stuff, and reading some psychology stuff.  I personally think it's important to keep "exercising" the muscle (plus I like learning), but some of my peers decompress altogether and switch to consuming high quality brain rot.  Anyway, the point being, if you've been going hard and suddenly you feel disappointed, or sad, or don't feel that "spark", or feel yourself struggling to even do a few lines of code, it is almost certainly burn out. I know some nerds are kind of hard on themselves, so don't beat yourself up if you feel this way. It happens to all of us (unless you're abusing narcotics to stay locked in).  Take this as a sign and use the opportunity to do something else. One day you'll be doing something and out of seemingly nowhere you'll feel that "spark" again and be like HOLY FUCK, I WANT TO CODE (or whatever you do).  Pic unrelated

🤖 [GEMINI 분석] 2026-04-05 17:45 KST
🔴 위협 점수: 9/10 (심각)

### 1. 현재 주요 사이버보안 트렌드
최근 사이버 위협은 npm 패키지 및 CI/CD 파이프라인을 겨냥한 **Supply Chain Attack**이 주류를 이루고 있으며, 특히 `Axios`와 같은 대중적인 라이브러리를 악용한 대규모 침투가 확인되었습니다. 또한, `OAuth` 기반의 **Device Code Phishing** 공격이 전년 대비 37배 급증하며 기업 계정 탈취의 주요 경로로 부상했습니다. AI 에이전트 및 멀티 에이전트 환경에서의 프롬프트 인젝션과 같은 새로운 공격 표면이 확장되고 있으며, 국가 배후의 해킹 그룹들이 지정학적 갈등을 틈타 Wiper 악성코드를 배포하는 등 파괴적인 공격이 지속되고 있습니다.

### 2. 주요 위협 행위자 / 캠페인
- **TeamPCP**: 유럽연합 집행위원회(EC) 및 29개 기관을 대상으로 한 대규모 클라우드 공급망 공격 수행.
- **Kimsuky**: 악성 LNK 파일을 활용한 파이썬 기반 백도어 유포 및 이력서/대북 정책 문서 위장 피싱 캠페인 지속.
- **TA416**: 유럽 정부 및 외교 기관을 타겟으로 `PlugX` 및 OAuth 기반 피싱 공격 수행.
- **Stardust Chollima**: `Axios` npm 패키지 공급망 공격을 통해 개발 환경 침투 시도.
- **Handala Hack**: 이란 연계 해킹 그룹으로, 의료 기술 기업(Stryker) 등을 대상으로 데이터 파괴(Wiper) 공격 수행.

### 3. 한국 관련 위협
- **Kimsuky 그룹**: 한국 기업 및 기관을 대상으로 이력서 및 대북 정책 문서로 위장한 악성 LNK 파일 유포 정황 포착 ([이스트시큐리티 알약](https://blog.alyac.co.kr/5743)).
- **랜섬웨어 공격**: `NetRunner` 그룹이 대한민국 자동차 부품 제조 기업의 인도 법인을 대상으로 랜섬웨어 공격 수행 ([AhnLab ASEC](https://asec.ahnlab.com/ko/93157)).
- **국세청 사칭**: 국세청 세무조사를 사칭한 피싱 메일을 통해 백도어 악성코드 유포 ([이스트시큐리티 알약](https://blog.alyac.co.kr/5741)).

### 4. 주요 CVE / 취약점
- **CVE-2026-20093**: Cisco IMC 및 SSM On-Prem, CVSS 9.8, 원격 코드 실행 가능, 긴급 패치 권고.
- **CVE-2026-35616**: FortiClient EMS, CVSS 9.8, 야생(in-the-wild)에서 활발히 악용 중.
- **CVE-2025-7741**: Yokogawa CENTUM VP, 권한 탈취 및 사용자 계정 수정 가능.
- **CVE-2026-20929**: Kerberos Authentication Relay, CNAME Abuse를 통한 인증 우회.
- **CVE-2025-30208**: Vite 프론트엔드 빌드 툴, 노출된 인스턴스 대상 악용 시도.

### 5. IOC 요약
- **CVE**:
    - CVE-2026-35616 ([TheHackerNews](https://thehackernews.com/2026/04/fortinet-patches-actively-exploited-cve.html))
    - CVE-2025-30208 ([SANS ISC](https://isc.sans.edu/diary/rss/32860))
    - CVE-2026-20929 ([Crowdstrike](https://www.crowdstrike.com/en-us/blog/detecting-kerberos-relay-attack-via-dns-cname-abuse/))
    - CVE-2026-20093 ([데일리시큐](https://www.dailysecu.com/news/articleView.html?idxno=206088))
    - CVE-2025-7741 ([CISA ICS](https://www.cisa.gov/news-events/ics-advisories/icsa-26-092-02))

### 6. 즉각 대응 권고
- **공급망 점검**: 프로젝트 내 `Axios` 및 기타 npm 패키지 버전 무결성 검증 및 최신 보안 패치 적용.
- **인증 강화**: `OAuth` Device Authorization Grant 흐름을 사용하는 애플리케이션의 접근 제어 정책 강화 및 비정상적인 로그인 패턴 모니터링.
- **패치 적용**: FortiClient EMS 및 Cisco IMC/SSM On-Prem 제품의 즉각적인 펌웨어/소프트웨어 업데이트 수행.

### 7. 위협 수준 평가
- **점수**: 9/10
- **근거**: 공급망 공격이 정부 기관 및 대규모 클라우드 환경을 직접 타격하고 있으며, 다수의 치명적 취약점(CVSS 9.8)이 야생에서 활발히 악용되고 있어 즉각적인 대응이 필수적임.
